worktree.teardown: bun run dev:stop && rm -f apps/server/solar.db apps/server/solar.db-shm apps/server/solar.db-wal

commitMessage.instructions:
Write a Conventional Commit message for the provided changes.

Output only the commit message—no preamble, explanation, Markdown fences, or commentary.

Required format:

<type>(<scope>): <concise imperative summary>

<body>

Rules:
- Use a valid Conventional Commit type: feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, test, build, ci, perf, or style.
- Always include a specific scope.
- Keep the title under 72 characters. Use imperative mood and describe the user-visible or primary change.
- Include a blank line after the title.
- The body is required and must contain 2–5 bullet points.
- Each bullet must describe a concrete final change, not a vague summary or restatement of the title.
- Cover, where applicable:
  - behavior added, changed, or fixed;
  - important implementation details;
  - persistence, configuration, API, or data-model changes;
  - migration/compatibility implications;
  - tests added or updated.
- Do not invent changes not supported by the input.
- Do not use generic phrases such as “update logic,” “improve handling,” “misc fixes,” or “persist settings” without stating what specifically changed.
- Do not mention the process of making the change (for example, “implemented,” “worked on,” or “this commit”).
- If only one meaningful change exists, still provide at least two bullets: one for the behavior and one for the implementation or affected area.

Example:

feat(chat): add per-model generation defaults

- Allow each chat model to define its own default generation parameters.
- Save model-specific defaults with persisted chat settings.
- Restore the matching defaults when loading an existing conversation.
- Preserve existing global defaults for models without an override.

pullRequest.instructions:
Write a pull request title and description for the final changes.

Format:
- First line: a concise, imperative PR title that summarizes the primary user-facing or technical outcome.
- Blank line.
- Body: a detailed Markdown description of the completed work.

Requirements:
- Explain what changed, why it changed, and how the relevant behavior, data flow, configuration, UI, or APIs now work.
- Enumerate meaningful implementation details, including related refactors, persistence/migration behavior, defaults, fallbacks, compatibility considerations, and error handling when applicable.
- Describe the resulting impact on users or developers.
- Use clear paragraphs and Markdown bullet lists where helpful.
- Base the description only on the provided changes; do not invent rationale, behavior, or validation.
- Do not include a “Testing”, “Tests”, “Validation”, checklist, or similarly named section.
- Do not mention that no tests were run.
- Avoid vague statements such as “various improvements” or “updated files.”
